1.benefit: Removal of physical ailments; purification of internal fire (Jatharagni)
1.description: Auspicious for house construction and travel.

2.benefit: Stable health and relief from chronic diseases
2.description: Favorable for ornaments, music, and Vastu Karma.

3.benefit: Marital bliss (Saubhagya) and desired life partner
3.description: Reading Gauri Kalyanam is highly recommended today.

5.benefit: Protection from toxins, serpents, and hidden enemies
5.description: Lending money on this day is traditionally avoided.

6.benefit: Victory over challenges and protection of children

7.benefit: Vitality, eye health, and spiritual radiance

8.benefit: Strength to overcome enemies and internal fear

9.benefit: Victory of Dharma and divine protection
9.description: Auspicious for Shakti Upasana and Rama Taraka Mantra.

10.benefit: Success in directions and vehicle-related prosperity
10.description: Excellent for beginning new business ventures.

11.description: Strict fasting (Nirjala or Phalahari) to purify past karmas.

13.benefit: Marital happiness and fulfillment of worldly desires
13.name: Pradosh Vrat (Twilight Worship)

14.benefit: Dissolution of negative habits and deep transformation

Kali Chaudas (Naraka Chaturdashi).description: Kali Chaudas worship of Goddess Kali and Hanuman during Sangava or Arunodaya

Naraka Chaturdashi Abhyanga Snan.description: Traditional pre-sunrise oil bath when Chaturdashi is present at Arunodaya
Naraka Chaturdashi Abhyanga Snan.rule_convention: Abhyanga Snan is resolved for the configured location; public almanacs can differ by one civil date.

Kali Puja.description: Festival of lights / Worship of Goddess Kali

Govardhan Puja.rule_convention: Govardhan Puja is resolved for the configured location; city-specific public almanacs can differ by one civil date.

Bhai Dooj.description: Brother-sister relationship celebration (known as Bhai Tika in Nepal)

Labh Pancham.description: Auspicious day for businesses in Gujarat

Chhath Puja (Sandhya Arghya).description: Major day (3rd of 4) of Chhath Puja - evening arghya to Surya; festival runs Kartika Shukla 4-7

Jalaram Jayanti.description: Birth anniversary of Saint Jalaram Bapa of Virpur

Jagaddhatri Puja.description: Worship of Goddess Jagaddhatri in Bengal

Subrahmanya Shashti (Champa Shashthi).description: Important festival dedicated to Lord Subrahmanya (Karthikeya) and Shiva

Pramukh Swami Maharaj Jayanti.description: Birth anniversary of Pramukh Swami Maharaj (7 December 1921, Magshar Sud 8, VS 1978)

Tulsi Vivah.description: Ceremonial marriage of Tulsi to Vishnu

Vaikuntha Chaturdashi.description: Sacred day for both Vishnu and Shiva worshippers

Dev Diwali (Tripurari Purnima).description: Festival of Varanasi celebrating Shiva's victory over Tripurasura; ghats illuminated with lamps

Karthigai Deepam.description: Tamil festival of lights; Maha Deepam lit at Tiruvannamalai

Guru Nanak Jayanti (Kartika Purnima).description: Birth anniversary of the first Sikh Guru; major pan-India observance

Vivah Panchami.description: Wedding anniversary of Rama and Sita

Mokshada Ekadashi (Geeta Jayanti).description: Fasting for Mokshada Ekadashi and celebration of Bhagavad Gita (Geeta Jayanti); known as the Gateway to Heaven

Thai Poosam.description: Tamil festival dedicated to Lord Murugan; observed when Pushya nakshatra coincides with Purnima in Tamil month Thai (Jan-Feb)

Onam (Thiruvonam).description: Kerala harvest festival; Thiruvonam nakshatra (Shravana) in Malayalam month Chingam (Aug-Sep); marks King Mahabali's annual visit

Vasant Panchami.description: Worship of Goddess Saraswati and welcoming of spring; Shikshapatri presentation

Holika Dahan.description: Bonfire ceremony, victory of good over evil

Phuldolotsava.description: Swaminarayan Pushpadolotsav / Fuldol tithi observance in the Holi/Purnima festival cluster
Phuldolotsava.rule_convention: Sect-specific tithi observance; public celebration logistics and mainstream post-Holi civil dates can differ.

Varalakshmi Vratam.description: Worship of Goddess Lakshmi for prosperity

Padmini Ekadashi.description: Special Ekadashi occurring ONLY during Adhik Maas

Parama Ekadashi.description: Special Ekadashi occurring ONLY during Adhik Maas Krishna Paksha

Karwa Chauth.description: Fasting by married women for the longevity of husbands

Yama Deepam.description: Lighting lamps to please Lord Yama

Chopda Pujan.description: Consecration of account books and Lakshmi worship

Bali Pratipada.description: Worship of King Bali and Govardhan Hill

Sata Yuga Diwas.description: Commemoration of the beginning of Satya Yuga

Treta Yuga Diwas.description: Commemoration of the beginning of Treta Yuga

Dwapara Yuga Diwas.description: Commemoration of the beginning of Dwapara Yuga

Kali Yuga Diwas.description: Commemoration of the beginning of Kali Yuga

Vaivaswata Manvadi.description: Commemoration of the beginning of Vaivaswata Manvantara

Swayambhuva Manvadi.description: Commemoration of the beginning of Swayambhuva Manvantara

Brahma Savarni Manvadi.description: Commemoration of the beginning of Brahma Savarni Manvantara

Daksha Savarni Manvadi.description: Commemoration of the beginning of Daksha Savarni Manvantara

Indra Savarni Manvadi.description: Commemoration of the beginning of Indra Savarni Manvantara

Tamasa Manvadi.description: Commemoration of the beginning of Tamasa Manvantara

Uttama Manvadi.description: Commemoration of the beginning of Uttama Manvantara

Raivata Manvadi.description: Commemoration of the beginning of Raivata Manvantara

Chakshusha Manvadi.description: Commemoration of the beginning of Chakshusha Manvantara

Swarochisha Manvadi.description: Commemoration of the beginning of Swarochisha Manvantara

Savarni Manvadi.description: Commemoration of the beginning of Savarni Manvantara

Rudra Savarni Manvadi.description: Commemoration of the beginning of Rudra Savarni Manvantara

Daiva Savarni Manvadi.description: Commemoration of the beginning of Daiva Savarni Manvantara

Bhishma Dwadashi.description: Dedicated to Bhishma Pitamaha of Mahabharata

Ranga Panchami.description: Festival of colors celebrated five days after Holi

Sheetala Ashtami.description: Worship of Goddess Sheetala; eating stale food (Basoda)

Ramakrishna Jayanti.description: Birth anniversary of Sri Ramakrishna Paramahansa

Aniruddha Chaturthi.description: Dedicated to Aniruddha, grandson of Lord Krishna

Pradyumna Chaturthi.description: Dedicated to Pradyumna, son of Lord Krishna

Thrissur Pooram.description: Grand festival of Thrissur, Kerala; observed during Medam month Pooram Nakshatra

Attukal Pongal.description: Large gathering of women for cooking offering to Attukal Amma

Rigveda Upakarma.description: Annual ritual of changing the sacred thread for Rigvedis

Yajurveda Upakarma.description: Annual ritual of changing the sacred thread for Yajurvedis

Samaveda Upakarma.description: Annual ritual of changing the sacred thread for Samavedis

Gayatri Japam.description: Chanting of Gayatri Mantra following Upakarma

Anvadhan.description: Fasting observed on Purnima/Amavasya; typically the day before Ishti

Ishti.description: Sacrificial rites performed on the day following Purnima/Amavasya

Kalashtami.description: Monthly fasting dedicated to Lord Bhairava

Masik Durgashtami.description: Monthly fasting dedicated to Goddess Durga

Masik Krishna Janmashtami.description: Monthly fasting marking the birth tithi of Lord Krishna

Chandra Darshana.description: First sighting of the moon after Amavasya

Bhogi Pandigai.description: First day of Pongal, celebrated in honor of Lord Indra

Makaravilakku.description: Annual festival held on Makara Sankranti in Sabarimala

Magh Bihu.description: Harvest festival of Assam, coinciding with Makara Sankranti

Thai Amavasai.description: Auspicious Amavasya in Tamil month of Thai for ancestor worship

Masik Karthigai.description: Monthly Krittika Nakshatra fasting for Lord Murugan

Janaki Jayanti.description: Birth anniversary of Goddess Sita (according to some regional calendars)

Ashoka Ashtami Vrat.description: Fasting and worship on Chaitra Shukla Ashtami, associated with Goddess Durga and Ashoka blossoms

Chitra Pournami.description: Full moon day associated with Chitra Nakshatra; worship of Chitragupta

Maha Bharani.description: Highly auspicious Bharani Nakshatra during Pitru Paksha

Maha Sangada Hara Chathurti.description: Most auspicious Sankashti Chaturthi of the year

Nagula Chavithi.description: Worship of Nagas in Andhra Pradesh

Varada Chaturthi.description: Dedicated to Lord Ganesha for fulfillment of desires

Yama Panchaka Begins.description: Start of the five-day Diwali celebration period

Mandala Pooja Begins.description: Beginning of 41-day Mandala period at Sabarimala

Mandala Pooja.description: Conclusion of 41-day Mandala period at Sabarimala

Hanuman Puja.description: Worship of Lord Hanuman during Diwali period

Navratri Begins.description: Start of the nine-day Navratri festival

Kedar Gauri Vrat.description: Vrata observed by married women during Diwali
